summary: Old Kongo stage decomp PR centered on src/melee/gr/groldkongo.c. It typed the Old Kongo parameter data blob, added missing imports, implemented grOldKongo_80210454 and grOldKongo_80210650, improved large stage state-machine functions grOldKongo_8020F888 and grOldKongo_802100FC, and updated groldkongo.h prototypes. The decomp-dev bot reported +800 matched code bytes and +112 matched data bytes, with new 100% matches for grOldKongo_80210454, grOldKongo_80210650, .sdata2, .sdata, and grOldKongo_8021005C. Most other touched files were formatting-only cleanup.
key_files: src/melee/gr/groldkongo.c - primary Old Kongo decomp, data typing, callback setup, and stage behavior implementations
src/melee/gr/groldkongo.h - replaced UNK prototypes for grOldKongo_80210454 and grOldKongo_80210650 with typed signatures
what_changed: Expanded the Old Kongo static parameter struct grOk_804D6A90 from padding into typed f32, s16, and s32 fields through x6C, enabling direct field access and data matching.
Added a static nine-word grOk_803B8408 struct used as stack/source data in the grOldKongo_8020F888 fighter interaction path.
Added imports for placeholder.h, baselib/debug.h, cm/camera.h, ef/efsync.h, ft/ftlib.h, and ftCommon/ftCo_BarrelWait.h to support the newly decompiled stage logic.

Changed grOldKongo_8020F52C from a call to Ground_SetupStageCallbacks into explicit setup: clearing Ground callbacks, installing grDisplay_801C5DB0 via GObj_SetupGXLink, assigning callback3, running callback0, and installing callback2 as a proc.

Replaced the placeholder for grOldKongo_8020F888 with a large partial C implementation covering rotation/state transitions, tau wrapping, animation control, material control, and fighter/barrel interaction via ftCo_8009EC70.
Completed grOldKongo_8021005C formatting/field access; the bot reported it moved from 99.76% to 100.00%.

Replaced the placeholder for grOldKongo_802100FC with logic that waits on a timer, starts an animation, positions a JObj at camera-dependent left/right bounds, and resets the timer after grAnime_801C83D0 completes.
Replaced the placeholder for grOldKongo_80210454 with a 100% matched function that checks fighter proximity, stores the fighter GObj, starts animation/material/effect work, and calls ftLib_80086C18.
Replaced the placeholder for grOldKongo_80210650 with a 100% matched weighted random angle selector using grOk_804D6A90 weights x2C through x3A.
Updated groldkongo.h so grOldKongo_80210454 is s32 grOldKongo_80210454(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*) and grOldKongo_80210650 is f32 grOldKongo_80210650(void).
Applied broad formatting-only edits in unrelated fighter, item, library, menu, and other stage files; no evidence in the diff suggests behavioral changes in those files.
smart_moves: Typed only the known offsets of grOk_804D6A90 while preserving x-offset field names, which improved code readability without inventing semantics and also helped .sdata/.sdata2 matching.
Manually expanded Ground_SetupStageCallbacks in grOldKongo_8020F52C, a useful tactic when the shared helper has the right semantics but not the exact call/order/codegen needed for a local match.
Kept grOldKongo_80210650 as a nested subtract-and-branch weighted random selection instead of abstracting it into a loop, preserving branch structure and exact radian constants.
Used squared distance comparison in grOldKongo_80210454 against grOk_804D6A90->x10 squared, matching the visible arithmetic pattern without introducing a sqrt helper.

Preserved codegen-sensitive details in grOldKongo_8020F888, including explicit temp variables, fallthrough/goto structure, f32/f64 casts for tau wrapping, exact constants, and stack locals sp10 through sp30.
Updated header prototypes immediately after identifying real signatures, making later callsites safer and reducing future UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Stage parameter blobs should be converted from padding to typed offset fields incrementally; this can unlock both code matches and data-section matches while avoiding premature semantic names.

A shared helper can be semantically correct but still block matching. For stage callback setup, hand-expanding the helper can expose the exact callback assignment and proc-install order expected by the original code.

Ground stage-state unions may still be under-modeled: this diff accesses oldkongo behavior through mixed gv.oldkongo, gv.arwing, gv.castle8, gv.castle11, and gv.castle2 overlays. Treat those as offset evidence, not final semantic naming.
Weighted random choices in decomp often need exact nested threshold code and exact float constants; replacing them with cleaner tables or loops may hurt matching.
Large partial matches can still be worthwhile when they isolate 100% subfunctions and improve global matched bytes, but future work should track the remaining mismatch separately.
The PR body and human review are empty, so the durable evidence is the diff plus the automated decomp-dev report; avoid inferring reviewer intent.
naming_conventions: Old Kongo functions keep address-style names such as grOldKongo_8020F888, grOldKongo_802100FC, grOldKongo_80210454, and grOldKongo_80210650 until semantic names are known.
File-level Old Kongo globals use the grOk_ prefix, shown by grOk_804D6A90, grOk_803E658C, and grOk_803B8408.
Unknown or partially understood struct fields retain offset names such as x0, x2, x4, x1C, x2C, x6C, and gv.oldkongo.xC6.
Project style places the pointer star next to the type: Fighter*, Ground_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, void*.
Inline helper names in nearby stage files use the function-address stem plus _inline, e.g. grFlatzone_80216F48_inline and grOldYoshi_8020EFCC_inline.
Constants/macros added for this file were named DegToRad and M_TAU; the implementation still uses exact literal radian constants where matching needs them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use __FILE__ in the OSReport call in grOldKongo_8020F52C rather than the literal old string, matching the diff's codegen-sensitive report expression.
Preserve explicit f64 promotion around tau wrapping: examples include (f64) angle +/- M_TAU and adding 1.5707963267948966 before casting back to f32.
Guard HSD_Randi calls against zero ranges with range != 0 ? HSD_Randi(range) : 0; this pattern appears repeatedly in the random timer/range code.
For grOldKongo_802100FC, keep the comma-expression HSD_Randf form visible in the diff: (2.0f * (0, HSD_Randf())) - 1.0f.

For grOldKongo_8020F888, keep explicit stack locals sp10 through sp30 and copy grOk_803B8408 fields before overwriting with grOk_804D6A90 values; this likely preserves stack layout and aggregate behavior.

Use exact game APIs rather than wrappers in the matched logic: Camera_800307D0, Ground_801C3FA4, Ground_801C4D70, Ground_801C4DA0, Ground_801C5440, lb_8000B1CC, grAnime_801C7FF8, grMaterial_801C9604, efSync_Spawn, ftLib_80086644, ftLib_80086C18, and ftCo_8009EC70.
Do not over-refactor grOldKongo_8020F888 yet: the bot reported it as an improvement to 91.05%, not a new 100% match.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so there is no author-written rationale beyond the branch/title and squash commit messages.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code at 65.27% (+0.02%, +800 bytes) and matched data at 38.61% (+0.01%, +112 bytes).
Bot-listed new 100% matches: grOldKongo_80210454 (+344 bytes), grOldKongo_80210650 (+304 bytes), main/melee/gr/groldkongo .sdata2 (+74 bytes), .sdata (+24 bytes), and grOldKongo_8021005C (+1 byte).
Bot-listed unmatched improvements: grOldKongo_8020F888 to 91.05% (+1821 bytes), grOldKongo_802100FC to 97.57% (+831 bytes), and .data to 36.33% (+13 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish grOldKongo_8020F888 beyond the 91.05% bot-reported state.
Verify whether grOldKongo_802100FC later reached 100%, since the visible bot report lists it at 97.57% despite squash-message wording suggesting a match attempt.
Audit and semantically name grOk_804D6A90 fields x0 through x6C after more Old Kongo behavior is understood.
Refine the Ground gv.oldkongo struct so grOldKongo_8020F888 no longer needs unrelated arwing/castle overlay field names for Old Kongo offsets.
Check whether the hand-expanded grOldKongo_8020F52C callback setup can be safely re-abstracted after matching is complete.
